circuits.compliance_note: TT lighting circuit. Zs = Ze + (R1+R2) = 200 + (2 × 12.1 mΩ/m × 18 m) = 200.436 Ω. RCD-derived Zs_max = U0/IΔn = 230/0.030 = 7666.67 Ω (per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5.3 RCD disconnection path). Ra·IΔn = 200 × 0.030 = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V satisfies §411.5.3 disconnection condition. Type AC sufficient — pure resistive/inductive lighting load, no DC residual expected.

TT kitchen socket-outlet circuit. Zs = 200 + (2 × 7.41 mΩ/m × 12 m) = 200.178 Ω. RCD-derived Zs_max = 7666.67 Ω. Ra·IΔn = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V per §411.5.3. Type A per §531.3.3 — domestic socket outlets host electronic loads (chargers, small appliances) with ≤6 mA DC residual; Type AC would be inadequate.

TT dedicated water-heater circuit. Zs = 200 + (2 × 7.41 mΩ/m × 6 m) = 200.089 Ω. Resistive storage-tank heater, but installed on socket-outlet spur — Type A retained as a precaution per §531.3.3. Ra·IΔn = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V.
TT bedroom socket-outlet circuit. Zs = 200 + (2 × 7.41 mΩ/m × 14 m) = 200.207 Ω. Type A per §531.3.3 (electronic loads expected). Ra·IΔn = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V.

assumptions: TT system — no PME, no utility metallic services. Single 3 m driven rod with installer-measured Ra = 200 Ω accepted as the design value (worst-case dry-season). Deterministic refinement via calc.electrode_resistance is deferred per WI3.
RCD disconnection condition per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5.3: Ra × IΔn ≤ 50 V. With Ra = 200 Ω and IΔn = 30 mA → 6.0 V ≤ 50 V passes with margin of ~8×.

RCD type selection per IEC 60364-4-41 §531.3.3: Type A on all socket-outlet circuits (F02-F04) to handle DC residual from electronic loads; Type AC on the dedicated lighting circuit F01 (no DC residual expected).
No extraneous-conductive-parts to bond — off-grid cottage has no incoming metallic water/gas service (rainwater + bottled LPG). Main bonding array is therefore empty by site fact, not by oversight.

Cable resistance values are LLM-estimates using IEC 60228 / Eland 20 °C Cu conductor data (1.5 mm²: 12.1 mΩ/m; 2.5 mm²: 7.41 mΩ/m). Deterministic refinement via calc.zs_loop_impedance is deferred per WI3. The conservative 20 °C base under-estimates resistance vs the 70 °C operating case by ~20 %; the design margin (Zs ≈ 200 Ω vs Zs_max 7667 Ω) is two orders of magnitude, so temperature uplift is immaterial to the disconnection-condition pass.

drawing_notes: TT system — single rod electrode E1 is the ONLY protective earth source. Cottage has no incoming metallic services; main bonding array intentionally empty.
All four final circuits protected by 30 mA RCD per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5. Lighting F01 = Type AC; socket-outlet circuits F02-F04 = Type A.
Disconnection condition Ra × IΔn = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V per §411.5.3 — passes with ~8× margin. Ra acceptance ceiling for the design: Ra_max = 50 / 0.030 = 1666 Ω, well above the measured 200 Ω.

Ra = 200 Ω is an installer-measured worst-case dry-season value. Periodic re-measurement (every 5 years per IEC 60364-6 §61.3.6 inspection cycle) is recommended; if Ra drifts above 1500 Ω the disconnection condition margin erodes and a supplementary rod or chemical electrode must be added.
flags: TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.electrode_resistance — Ra=200Ω is installer-measured (engineer-input); deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.
TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance — Zs values are LLM-estimates from 20°C Cu data; deterministic refinement deferred per WI3.

rationale.chat_summary: TT earthing for an INT rural off-grid cottage (single-phase 230 V, no utility PEN bond). Single 3 m driven rod at Ra=200 Ω is the ONLY system earth. All four final circuits protected by 30 mA RCD per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5 — Type AC on lighting, Type A on socket circuits per §531.3.3. Ra·IΔn = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V passes the §411.5.3 disconnection condition. Reply to refine, e.g. 'add solar PV submain'.

sections.title: Earthing System
sections.summary: TT mandated by site fact: off-grid cottage has no utility supply, therefore no PEN bond and no TN-arrangement is possible. The inverter-side neutral-earth bond establishes the system as TT, with the consumer-side rod E1 acting as both the system earth and the protective earth.

decisions.label: TT system — off-grid mandates it
decisions.summary: No grid connection → no utility-side bond → TT is the only valid IEC 60364-4-41 system class. The inverter creates one neutral-earth bond at the consumer side; all fault current returns to that bond via the local rod electrode and the earth.

sections.title: Main Earthing Terminal
sections.summary: MET at the cottage meter board, kitchen utility area. 16 mm² Cu main earthing conductor sized per IEC 60364-5-54 §542.3 — TT installation with single rod, so the MEC carries the same fault path as the rod tail; 16 mm² is the IEC 60364-5-54 §542.3.1 minimum for Cu MEC where buried portion is mechanically protected and corrosion-resistant.

decisions.label: MET location at kitchen meter board
decisions.summary: Co-located with the consumer unit for short bond run and accessible inspection per §542.4.1.

decisions.label: 16 mm² Cu main earthing conductor
decisions.summary: TT with single rod — MEC sized to §542.3.1 minimum for mechanically protected, corrosion-resistant Cu.

sections.title: Electrodes
sections.summary: Single 3 m × 16 mm copper-clad steel driven rod E1, installed in sandy loam at 800 Ω·m soil resistivity. Installer-measured Ra = 200 Ω at handover (dry-season worst case). Ra is the design value pending calc.electrode_resistance tool execution. The acceptance ceiling for the chosen RCD (30 mA) is Ra_max = 50 V / 0.030 A = 1666 Ω — the measured 200 Ω passes with ~8× margin.

decisions.label: Single 3 m driven rod E1
decisions.summary: One rod is sufficient because the Ra·IΔn ≤ 50 V condition is met with 8× margin at 30 mA. Adding a second rod would reduce Ra further but is not required to achieve disconnection compliance.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5.3 — RCD disconnection condition Ra·IΔn ≤ 50 V

decisions.label: Periodic re-measurement
decisions.summary: Ra in sandy loam drifts with seasonal moisture. IEC 60364-6 §61.3.6 mandates periodic verification; re-measure every 5 years and add a supplementary rod if Ra exceeds 1500 Ω (which would erode the disconnection margin).

sections.title: Main Bonding
sections.summary: No main protective bonds — the cottage has no incoming metallic services (rainwater supply, bottled LPG). The empty main_bonding array is a site fact, not an oversight. If a future borehole water pump introduces an incoming metallic pipe, that pipe must be bonded to MET per §544.1.

sections.title: Supplementary Bonding
sections.summary: No supplementary bonding zones declared. The cottage has no bathroom (washroom is a dry-composting WC outside the equipotential zone) and no other §701/§702/§710 special-location triggers. If a future build adds a wet bathroom, IEC 60364-7-701 supplementary bonding will be required at that zone.

sections.title: CPC + Zs Verification
sections.summary: Each final circuit has its CPC sized per IEC 60364-5-54 Table 54.2 (S=CPC for S≤16 mm² in Cu — the 1.5 / 2.5 mm² regime). Zs is computed as Ze + (R1+R2) where Ze=Ra=200 Ω dominates and the cable R1+R2 contribution is sub-1 Ω. Compliance is via the §411.5.3 RCD disconnection path: Zs ≤ U0/IΔn = 230/0.030 = 7666.67 Ω is satisfied by every circuit with three orders of magnitude of margin.

decisions.label: CPC = line per Table 54.2 (S ≤ 16 mm²)
decisions.summary: IEC 60364-5-54 Table 54.2 requires CPC ≥ line CSA for line ≤ 16 mm² in Cu. F01-F04 all satisfy this with CPC = line size.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-5-54 Table 54.2 — CPC sizing for S ≤ 16 mm² Cu

decisions.label: Zs verification by RCD disconnection path
decisions.summary: TT with high-Ra rod electrode — OCPD alone (B6/B16 MCB) cannot meet §411.4 disconnection time because Ze = 200 Ω yields an earth-fault current of just ~1.15 A (230/200), well below any MCB instantaneous trip. The §411.5.3 RCD path replaces the OCPD path: Zs ≤ U0/IΔn = 7666.67 Ω, satisfied by every circuit (Zs ≈ 200.1–200.4 Ω).
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5 — TT disconnection by RCD; Zs_max = U0/IΔn

sections.title: RCD Requirements
sections.summary: TT mandates RCD on every final circuit per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5. All four circuits take 30 mA. Type selection per §531.3.3: Type AC on the dedicated lighting circuit F01 (no DC residual expected from B6 + filament/halogen/LED-driver loads); Type A on F02-F04 socket-outlet circuits (≤6 mA DC residual tolerance accommodates electronic chargers, small appliances). The disconnection condition Ra × IΔn = 200 × 0.030 = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V passes with margin of ~8× (action threshold 50 V).

decisions.label: 30 mA RCD on every final circuit (TT mandate)
decisions.summary: IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5 mandates RCD-based ADS in TT. 30 mA chosen because it gives 8× headroom on the disconnection condition AND meets the additional-protection requirement §415.1.1 for general-purpose use in domestic.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5 — TT RCD mandate; §415.1.1 — 30 mA additional protection

decisions.label: Type AC on F01 lighting; Type A on F02-F04 sockets
decisions.summary: Type AC adequate where no DC residual is expected (lighting). Type A required on socket circuits per §531.3.3 — domestic socket-outlets accommodate electronic loads with ≤6 mA DC residual which would blind a Type AC RCD.
decisions.rule: IEC 60364-4-41 §531.3.3 — RCD type selection by load DC characteristics

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Compliant — all four circuits pass the §411.5.3 RCD disconnection condition with ~8× margin on Ra·IΔn and three orders of magnitude on Zs. No non-compliance flags. Two TOOL-CALL-PENDING entries remain (calc.electrode_resistance for live Ra refinement, calc.zs_loop_impedance for deterministic Zs).

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: Five assumptions captured: TT system mandated by off-grid context; installer-measured Ra=200 Ω accepted as worst-case design value with WI3 tool deferral; RCD selectivity is single-stage (no upstream RCD); no extraneous metallic services exist on site; cable R1+R2 estimated at 20 °C with the resulting under-estimate immaterial given the two-orders-of-magnitude Zs margin.

invariants.evidence: Jurisdiction-system match: INT + TT requires ≥1 electrode per the validator INV-02 table. electrodes[] has one entry (E1, single 3 m driven rod) satisfying the rule.

CPC sizing method per jurisdiction: INT permits {iec60364_table_54.2, iec60364_adiabatic_543.1.2}. All four circuits use iec60364_table_54.2 because line CSA is ≤16 mm² Cu (1.5 or 2.5 mm²), so Table 54.2 applies (CPC=line).

TT system → rcd_required is true on all 4 final circuits (F01-F04) per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5. Disconnection condition §411.5.3: Ra·IΔn = 200 × 0.030 = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V passes with ~8× margin. RCD type selection per §531.3.3: Type AC on lighting F01, Type A on socket circuits F02-F04.

Tool deferral shape: tool_call_pending_for_zs=true and zs_calc_tool_input is present and well-formed (jurisdiction=INT, supply.ze_declared_ohm=200, supply.system_type=TT, 4 circuit entries). flags[] contains TOOL-CALL-PENDING:calc.zs_loop_impedance string per INV-09 critical pairing.

Jurisdiction is INT, not KE, so the 'KS 1700' string MUST NOT appear in any citation. Confirmed: no code_clause field contains 'KS 1700'. All citations are 'IEC 60364-X-XX:YYYY §N.N.N' form per the INT citation contract.
